articles.title: The Best Cheap Desktop PCs for 2026
articles.description: You don't need to spend four figures to get a reliable home PC—many of our budget desktop favorites are under $600. Check out our top labs-tested picks among conventional towers, mini PCs, all-in-ones, gaming rigs, and more.

articles.title: Top fertiliser companies float tender to buy 600k t Sulphur
articles.description: Top Indian fertiliser firms have united to buy a large quantity of sulphur. This critical raw material is needed for phosphatic fertilisers. The collective move signals preparedness for ongoing global supply disruptions and rising prices. India relies heavily on imports for key fertilisers and their components.

articles.title: Chinese Proverb of the Day: “The tongue of a woman is the... — Life lessons on communication, influence, perception, and why you should think before you speak
articles.description: Chinese Proverb of the Day highlights the meaning of “The tongue of a woman is the sword that is never allowed to rust.” The saying reflects how speech and communication are often seen as powerful forces that influence emotions, opinions, and relationships in everyday life. It suggests that words are always active and carry a strong impact depending on how they are used in different situations.

articles.title: Stocks in news: Lenskart, Apollo Hospitals, ZEE, BPCL, Hindalco
articles.description: Indian markets navigated a sideways trend, with analysts urging caution until the 24,000 mark is reclaimed. Key stocks like Lenskart, Apollo Hospitals, ZEE, BPCL, and Hindalco are in focus due to Q4 results and news. BEL reported a profit rise, while ZEE saw a net loss. Maruti Suzuki expanded production capacity.

articles.title: Cement cos diverge on expansion plans amid West Asia uncertainty
articles.description: Indian cement companies are taking different paths on spending. Some, like Ultratech Cement, Dalmia Bharat, and Nuvoco Vistas, are increasing investments for expansion. Others, including Ambuja Cement and Shree Cement, are slowing down their capital expenditure. This divergence reflects varied growth strategies in the Indian market. The focus remains on India's long-term development drivers.

articles.title: Rashee Rice arrested and jailed again for marijuana, will miss Chiefs OTAs from jail
articles.description: Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Rashee Rice begins a 30-day jail sentence for violating probation after testing positive for THC. This follows a March 2024 incident where Rice caused a six-car crash and fled the scene. The absence means he'll miss crucial team activities, impacting his development and the Chiefs' offense. Rice also faces ongoing civil lawsuits.

articles.title: Jack Ryan star John Krasinski says Indian films are “cutting-edge” amid Dhurandhar buzz: "Blown away by films coming out from over there"
articles.description: Jack Ryan star John Krasinski praised Indian cinema while reacting to Ranveer Singh’s blockbuster Dhurandhar. The Hollywood actor also expressed interest in watching the globally popular spy-action film.

articles.title: Massive crackdown in J-K: Crores in Property seized, 7 peddlers arrested
articles.description: Under the ongoing Nasha Mukt Jammu and Kashmir Abhiyaan, J&K Police carried out a series of anti-drug operations in Pulwama, Baramulla, Handwara and Anantnag districts, leading to the arrest of seven drug peddlers and the seizure of contraband substances.

articles.title: UK railway bridge famous for smashing vehicles destroys tractor roof again
articles.description: A tractor's roof was ripped off after getting stuck under a notoriously low railway bridge in Ely, Cambridgeshire, a site with a history of vehicle collisions. Despite clear height warnings and a nearby level crossing, drivers repeatedly misjudge their vehicle's height. This incident adds to the bridge's infamous reputation, though no trains were disrupted and no one was injured.

articles.title: Unbelievable Twist: Farmer Accidentally Becomes “Owner” of a Train! Read Details
articles.description: We often hear of people owning cars, buses, or jets, but a train? A farmer from Punjab reportedly ended up as the “owner” of a train due to a surprising Indian Railways blunder. Here’s what actually happened in this bizarre case.

articles.title: Beyond hotels and restaurants: Why food technology is quietly becoming one of India's hottest career choices
articles.description: Telangana's booming food sector, encompassing processing, agri-tech, and exports, has spurred demand for skilled professionals. The state's higher education council has launched a new BBA in Food Technology, Food Security, and Agriculture, integrating science, business, and policy. This interdisciplinary program aims to equip graduates for evolving industry needs, bridging the gap between academic knowledge and practical market demands.

articles.title: Fuel price hike: Delhi-NCR to see 3-day strike by cab, auto drivers starting Thursday
articles.description: Commercial vehicle drivers in Delhi-NCR are striking May 21-23, demanding an immediate taxi and auto fare hike. Unions cite 15 years of stagnant fares despite soaring fuel and operational costs, and allege exploitation by app-based aggregators. A protest is planned for May 23, with threats of further escalation if demands aren't met.

articles.title: Wealthtech platform Trackk raises $3.7 million led by Lightspeed Ventures
articles.description: Trackk, a stock trading platform for Gen Z investors, has secured $3.7 million in a funding round. Lightspeed Ventures led the investment. The platform uses AI for stock discovery and simplified trading. Trackk aims to expand its product offerings and marketing efforts with this capital.
